column1: Deployment Checklist — External Parties, organized by functional area
External checklist for standard + standalone stations, i.e. where instrumental is responsible for desiging, procuring, and installing hardware, MES, image streams, data streams

column3: Obtained/provided all necessary documentation for security and internet clearance
Ease and location of factory entry / exit
Customer / CM Oversight necessity of IN employees
Supervisors, leads, and operators aware of deployment timing
Necessary personnel should be involved in alignment/kick off meeting
Clear dates, actions, and owners for any open items with defined cadence for follow up and owner accountability
Line will be fully stopped during installation
Shift schedule and ability to work after hours
Understanding requirements for: Eye glasses, steel toed shoes (+necessary ground rating required), coat, ear coverings
Physical access to station location guaranteed
Installation location cleared and evidence provided
Conveyed distance from the following: (If possible have electrical and layout drawing)
Installation vs production intent location representativeness
Space constraints reviewed (no interference with operators)
Confirm ability to bring adapters in case of internet down
Date to close with actions and owners defined, with appropriate cadence of follow up and escalation levels
Required IP addresses provided to customer IT and Instrumental TPM / FDE
Network speed sufficient for high-res image transfer
Correct cable type available (CAT6 or higher) or escalated to Instrumental TPM
Cable length sufficient for actual layout (avoid short cable issue)
Designated workspace to store and use equipment / tools
Access for moving equipment to line confirmed
Required tools available onsite (or brought by team)
Test sample ready to be used for setup and inspection
Acceptance criteria and type (SAT vs FAT) agreed and quantitatively defined (what = “working”)
CM: Key POC, IT POC, Process engineer, PM, Manager
Escalation path defined by type of issue and level of escalation
Language / shift coordination considered (if global site)
Anticipating risks and proactive mitigation prior to onsite presence
